description: CLI can load config from ~/.code-brain/config.yaml or CODE_BRAIN_CONFIG with sane defaults
steps: Delete any temporary test config file
Run `npm run cli -- doctor` in a clean environment
Verify the command prints resolved config paths and does not crash
Create a custom config file and set CODE_BRAIN_CONFIG to that path
Run `npm run cli -- doctor` again and verify the custom path is reported

description: Project registry can register and list multiple projects in config and index
steps: Run `npm run cli -- project register --id kilo-code --root ~/work/kilo-code --remote github.com/example/kilo-code`
Run `npm run cli -- project register --id code-brain --root ~/work/code-brain`
Run `npm run cli -- project list`
Verify both projects appear with ids, roots, and remotes
Inspect config file and verify both registrations were persisted

description: SQLite index database initializes with required tables and WAL mode
steps: Run `npm run cli -- doctor` with a writable config directory
Verify the index database file is created
Open the database with sqlite tooling or an automated test
Verify projects, pages, page_scopes, page_links, timeline_entries, ingest_events, and pages_fts exist
Verify `PRAGMA journal_mode` reports WAL

description: CLI exposes a stable command surface for doctor, project register, and project list
steps: Run `npm run cli -- --help`
Verify top-level commands include doctor and project
Run `npm run cli -- project --help`
Verify register and list subcommands are documented
Run each command with minimal valid arguments and verify non-error completion

description: Brain repo directories can be created on demand from config defaults or explicit paths
steps: Point config to a non-existent brain repo path
Run `npm run cli -- doctor`
Verify the repo root directory is created automatically
Verify the index database parent directory is created automatically
Verify no unrelated directories are created

description: Markdown page model can parse required frontmatter fields for issue, architecture, decision, practice, and change pages
steps: Create sample markdown pages for each page type under a temporary brain repo
Run the future parser entrypoint or automated tests
Verify valid pages are accepted
Verify missing required fields are rejected with a clear error
Verify page type specific templates remain distinguishable

description: Write-through sync writes Markdown first and refreshes SQLite index immediately after
steps: Create or update a page through the future upsert flow
Verify the markdown file exists with updated content
Immediately query the SQLite index for the same slug
Verify the indexed record matches the markdown state
Restart the process and verify the record is still discoverable

description: Reindex can rebuild SQLite from Markdown truth source after manual file edits
steps: Create a markdown page in the brain repo
Run the future `reindex` command
Verify the page appears in search or list output
Edit the markdown file by hand
Run `reindex` again and verify the index reflects the new content

description: Search can resolve project from explicit project first and context_path second
steps: Register two projects with different roots
Run the future search command with an explicit project and a mismatched context path
Verify the explicit project wins
Run search without project but with a valid context path
Verify the inferred project matches the path root

description: Search can combine Chinese and English query terms with scope and type filters
steps: Seed the brain repo with pages containing Chinese text, English symbols, and scope refs
Run the future search command using a mixed-language query
Verify the expected issue or practice page is returned
Run the same query with `types` filter
Verify the filtered result set narrows correctly

description: Search keeps a single API while supporting LLM quality-first reranking and local fallback
steps: Configure one LLM provider preset and seed multiple near-duplicate pages
Run the future search command with LLM enabled
Verify the best long-lived page ranks ahead of related change pages
Disable or break LLM access and run the same search
Verify the command still returns results using local fallback ordering

description: get_page and list_pages can retrieve canonical knowledge pages from the brain repo
steps: Seed the brain repo with multiple pages across types
Run the future `list_pages` operation for a single project
Verify only matching project pages are listed
Run the future `get_page` operation for one slug
Verify the returned page content matches the markdown source

description: record_change creates or updates a change page from diff, commit message, or agent summary
steps: Prepare a sample commit message and agent summary
Run the future `record_change` command with those inputs
Verify a change markdown page is created under the project changes path
Run the command again with the same source reference
Verify the existing change page is updated rather than duplicated

description: Fingerprint and source_ref dedupe prevent exact duplicate ingest events
steps: Submit the same change payload twice with identical project, source_ref, change_kind, and primary scope refs
Inspect the future ingest event records
Verify only one fingerprint entry is accepted
Change one dedupe input and submit again
Verify a new ingest event is created

description: Automatic long-lived knowledge upsert works in both rule mode and LLM mode
steps: Prepare one bugfix change payload and one practice-like summary payload
Run the future record_change flow with LLM disabled
Verify conservative linking or candidate creation happens without fabricating pages
Run the same flow with LLM enabled
Verify issue or practice pages are linked or updated more precisely

description: Link graph can connect change pages to issue, decision, architecture, and practice pages
steps: Create one change page and at least two long-lived pages
Run the future `link_pages` operation
Run the future `get_links` operation for the change page
Verify outgoing links and relation labels are stored
Verify reverse lookup can also discover related pages

description: MCP server exposes the same operations as the CLI without behavior drift
steps: Start the future MCP server locally
Invoke search and record_change through MCP
Invoke the equivalent CLI commands
Compare output structures and side effects
Verify both paths share the same validation rules

description: Provider presets support GLM, Qwen, MiniMax, DeepSeek, Kimi, and OpenAI-compatible routing
steps: Configure provider presets in config.yaml
Run the future config validation path
Verify each preset is accepted with base_url, api_key_env, and default_model
Select different providers for search and extract routing
Verify the resolved runtime config reflects the routing choices

description: Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and Gemini CLI can all follow the same search then record loop
steps: Prepare the documented rule or instruction snippets for each agent
Simulate one search call and one record_change call through each integration path
Verify Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex can use MCP or CLI as documented
Verify Gemini CLI can complete the loop through the CLI path
Verify all four leave knowledge in the same brain repo and index

description: Core tests cover config loading, project resolution, SQLite init, search fallback, and record_change upsert behavior
steps: Run `npm run test`
Verify automated tests execute against temporary directories
Verify tests cover config loading and project registry behavior
Verify tests cover SQLite initialization and fallback behavior
Verify tests fail if one of those contracts regresses

description: CLI help text is concise, readable, and aligned with the DESIGN.md vocabulary
steps: Run `npm run cli -- --help` and `npm run cli -- project --help`
Verify command names match DESIGN.md terminology such as search, get_page, and record_change
Verify examples are short and realistic
Verify required options are obvious
Verify help output stays readable in a standard terminal width

description: Config examples and quickstart docs make the happy path obvious for a single-user local setup
steps: Read the local setup documentation and sample config
Follow the documented steps in a clean temp environment
Verify a new user can initialize config and project registration without guesswork
Verify Chinese LLM provider presets are explained without assuming OpenAI parity
Verify the docs distinguish minimal config from advanced config

description: Errors carry context and tell the user what to fix next
steps: Run a command with a missing required option
Run a command with an invalid config value
Run a command with an unreadable path
Verify each error message names the failing input and next action
Verify stack traces are not dumped for expected user mistakes

description: Progress tracking files make future agent sessions easy to resume
steps: Open feature_list.json, claude-progress.txt, CLAUDE.md, and AGENTS.md
Verify the workflow explains how to resume work in a fresh session
Verify feature_list.json is ordered from foundational work to integrations
Verify progress notes identify the next recommended feature
Verify the files do not contradict the current repository state
